aspects.label: Described as: Stewards of the mysteries of God
Described as: Defenders of the faith
Described as: The servants of Christ’s people
Labours of, vain, without God’s blessing
Should be: Sober, just, and temperate
Should be: Strict in ruling their own families
Should be: Affectionate to their people
Should be: Ensample to the flock
Should not be: Lords over God’s heritage
Should not be: Greedy of filthy lucre
Should not be: Entangled by cares
Should not be: Given to wine
Should seek the salvation of their flock
Should make full proof of their ministry
Are bound to: Preach the gospel to all
Are bound to: Feed the Church
Are bound to: Build up the Church
Are bound to: Watch for souls
Are bound to: Pray for their people
Are bound to: Strengthen the faith of their people
Are bound to: War a good warfare
Should preach: According to the oracles of God
Should preach: Not with enticing words of man’s wisdom
Should preach: Not setting forth themselves
Should preach: Fully, and without reserve
Should preach: With plainness of speech
Should preach: With good will and love
Should preach: Without charge, if possible
Woe to those who do not preach the gospel
When faithful: Approve themselves as the ministers of God
When faithful: Thank God for his gifts to their people
When faithful: Glory in their people
When faithful: Rejoice in the faith and holiness of their people
When faithful: Commend themselves to the consciences of men
When unfaithful: Deal treacherously with their people
Their people are bound, to: Regard them as God’s messengers
Their people are bound, to: Not to despise them
Their people are bound, to: Attend to their instructions
Their people are bound, to: Follow their holy example
Their people are bound, to: Imitate their faith
Their people are bound, to: Hold them in reputation
Their people are bound, to: Love them
Their people are bound, to: Pray for them
Their people are bound, to: Obey them
Their people are bound, to: Give them joy
Their people are bound, to: Help them
Their people are bound, to: Support them
